coverage_explanation: The existing eucn:Spirit class already captures the core discriminating criteria for CN heading 2208: undenatured ethyl alcohol with ABV less than 80% vol, spirits, liqueurs, and other spirituous beverages. The hasValue restriction on cnHeadingCode ('2208') anchors the class to this heading. The decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent (less than 80% vol) captures the numeric threshold. The someValuesFrom restriction on producedBy (BFO_0000015 Process) captures the 'prepared by distillation or other process' criterion at a high level. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (1) the legal text specifies 'generally intended for human consumption', which could be modelled via eucn:isForDirectConsumption but is already partially implied by eucn:Beverage ancestry; (2) the specific enumeration of preparation methods (direct distillation with or without added flavouring, or by other means) is only partially covered by the generic producedBy restriction — a dedicated DistillationProcess class or subclasses thereof would give finer coverage; (3) the distinction between spirits/liqueurs/other spirituous beverages as sub-types is not further resolved at this heading level. The note text provided is also truncated ('entweder unmittelbar durch Des...'), limiting full analysis.
